steps.title: Identify the intersection of the two graphed lines
steps.content:
The graph displays two lines with different slopes (one positive, one negative), so they intersect at exactly one point.

Reading from the grid: the line with positive slope passes through $(0, -4)$ and $(6, -2)$, giving slope $\frac{1}{3}$.

The line with negative slope passes through $(0, 0)$ and $(6, -2)$, giving slope $-\frac{1}{3}$.

Both lines pass through the point $(6, -2)$, which is their intersection.

steps.title: Test whether the third line passes through the intersection point
steps.content:
For the system of three equations to have a solution, all three lines must meet at the same point. Substitute $(6, -2)$ into the third equation $x - 6y = -36$:
$$6 - 6(-2) = 6 + 12 = 18$$
Since $18 \neq -36$, the point $(6, -2)$ does not lie on the third line. The first two lines intersect at one point, but the third line passes through a different point. There is no location where all three lines meet simultaneously. That means the system has zero solutions — choice A is correct.
